raw_comment:
(Cont'd)
While I know that the first day of Spring is determined by the vernal equinox, which is when the sun crosses over the plane of the earth’s equator making night and day approximately equal lengths around the world, what effects might global warming have on the official first day of Spring? How might this graph be improved to showcase the correlation between when a state sees its first leaf and the official first day of Spring, as well as how the first day might change (i.e., occur sooner or later) due to the rising temperature of our earth’s atmosphere? 

Headline: Warmer Weather on the Way: When Will Spring Surface in Your Neck of the Woods?
ace_sentences: The first day of Spring is determined by the vernal equinox.
The vernal equinox is when the sun crosses over the plane of the earth’s equator.
At the vernal equinox, night and day have approximately equal lengths around the world.
Global warming might affect the official first day of Spring.
The improved graph can showcase the correlation between when a state sees its first leaf and the official first day of Spring.
The improved graph can showcase how the first day of Spring might change due to the rising temperature of our earth’s atmosphere.
The first day of Spring might occur sooner due to the rising temperature of our earth’s atmosphere.
The first day of Spring might occur later due to the rising temperature of our earth’s atmosphere.
The headline is Warmer Weather on the Way: When Will Spring Surface in Your Neck of the Woods?
